task_statement: P1c raw evidence stream secret/PII/provider-session scrub repair using the Smith-confirmed B standard phase graph: Codex+Claude design fan-out, design fan-in synthesis, design QA, COO planning gate, development, hard QA fan-out, and closure. This retries P1 after 0701b reached work/axis/evidence QA but stopped at code-QA adapter guard because local CLI output contained provider-session-like text. Use official build()/brick build route only; no new engine, --large, support-owned authority, or preset-only habit. All lanes must keep literal synthetic sensitive fixture values out of outputs and commands.

nodes.work_statement:
Codex design lane for P1c. Read the P1 phase doc, adopted audit refs, P1b graph/evidence summary, and active raw writer/checker surfaces. Focus on code surface, checker-first feasibility, write_scope, exact file/test boundaries, and how to prevent raw evidence secret/PII/provider-session-like payload persistence. Design only; do not edit files. Return reading_scope_map, invariants, checker/verifier plan, risk list, and not_proven.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Claude design lane for P1c. Independently read the P1 phase doc, adopted audit refs, P1b failure summary, and boundary docs. Focus on architecture, Brick/Agent/Link ownership, product/customer comprehension, failure scenarios, anti-bandaid risk, and policy questions around block vs redact vs mark. Design only; do not edit files. Return independent design evidence and not_proven; do not rely on Codex design.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Design fan-in synthesis. Read both independent design returns and synthesize conflicts, common ground, missing evidence, policy questions, not_proven, and a bounded execution contract for P1. This is synthesis only, not approval, quality judgment, source truth, or Movement authority. The output should make the downstream design-QA and work nodes cheaper and less exploratory.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Planning/design QA. Review the design-synthesis against P1 requirements and the standard phase graph rules. Ask only whether the design is safe to send to build(): Brick evidence sufficient? Agent lane/write_scope correct? Link Movement/target not stolen by support? Checkers actually cover the requirement? No new raw/secret/provider/dashboard/release risk? Return observations, missing/mismatched facts, boundary violations, and not_proven only.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

COO planning gate as a closure-synthesis Brick. Read design-synthesis and design-QA. Produce the bounded work contract and HOLD questions. If evidence is missing, record not_proven/remaining_delta instead of approving. Do not edit files; do not judge quality or choose Movement. This node carries the planning contract into development.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Implement P1 only using the COO gate/design handoff. Repair raw evidence stream persistence so credential/secret/PII/provider-session-like payloads cannot silently enter raw BRICK JSONL streams. Add checker-first negative probes covering active raw stream writer seams for at least raw/brick-work.jsonl, raw/agent-received.jsonl, raw/agent-return.jsonl, and raw/adapter-error.jsonl or active equivalents. Preserve Brick/Agent/Link boundaries: Support/evidence integrity only; no source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, Movement authority, scheduler/queue/retry, new axis, or broad product release/dashboard hardening. Preserve evidence refs, proof_limits, and not_proven when body detail is blocked/removed. Do not recursively ban ordinary nested words such as status/result in non-secret evidence. Run focused raw scrub profile, related focused profile(s), py_compile, and git diff --check. Include P1b work changed-file candidates as prior support evidence only, not source truth.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Code attack QA. Attack the P1 implementation with independent QA-built probes, not the builder fixtures. Drive real writer/checker surfaces and at least one caller-facing entry surface/profile. Probe category classes for credential-like, private-key-like, PII-like, and provider-session-like payloads without printing literals. Verify allowed non-secret evidence still persists with proof_limits and refs. Return observed facts and not_proven only.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Axis attack QA. Inspect whether P1 remains Support/evidence integrity only and does not make Support own Brick work truth, Agent return truth, Link Movement, success, or quality. Confirm P1 does not accidentally implement P4 AgentFact authority-key closure or P7 release/dashboard hardening. Return local non-binding observations only; no Link-facing Movement.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Evidence integrity QA. Inspect persisted raw/claim/evidence outputs and checker proof. Verify negative probes cannot pass by simply dropping required raw evidence. Verify refs, proof_limits, and not_proven remain useful after any block/remove behavior. Record stale-source/checker-overclaim risks. Return evidence observations only.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.

Final P1c closure synthesis. Fan in work plus all QA bodies. Separate observed_evidence, narrowly_proven, not_proven, remaining_delta, parent_goal_delta_status, next_target_candidates, deferred Smith/COO review queue, and transition_concern_evidence. Do not call overall goal complete. If P1 remains unproven, return HOLD/remaining_delta as support evidence only. Only this closure may emit Link-facing transition_concern_evidence, and it must be non-binding and literal-free.

STRICT OUTPUT HYGIENE: Do not echo, print, cat, return, or include literal synthetic secret/credential/PII/provider-session fixture values in any Agent return, command output summary, transition concern, or diagnostic excerpt. Commands must be written so their stdout/stderr contains only category labels, opaque probe ids, hashes, counts, file/evidence refs, and PASS/FAIL-style observations. If a probe would display a literal, suppress/redirect it and report only category/ref/hash.
